name: Autonomous urban passenger-miles share
whyItMatters: Tests whether city-first deployment timing in the thesis is directionally right.
statusPlaceholder: Internal log: operator filings and third-party mile estimates; baseline to be set each quarter.
interpretation: Faster share gain in target metros is consistent with operator scale economics; flat or declining share after permit expansion points to unit-economics or execution constraints rather than regulation alone.

name: Accident frequency — ADS vs. human baseline
whyItMatters: Underpins whether liability and insurance assumptions in the thesis are grounded.
statusPlaceholder: Awaiting comparable cohort update; no single public series is sufficient—use multiple sources.
interpretation: Persistent ADS disadvantage on like-for-like exposure would extend adoption and liability timelines; convergence or superiority supports faster pool repricing.

name: Personal-auto combined ratio trajectory
whyItMatters: Maps technology and loss-cost trends to P&C earnings and reserve behavior.
statusPlaceholder: Tracked at each sector earnings round; weather and severity noise require multi-period view.
interpretation: Structural improvement without a plausible frequency story while AV miles rise would weaken the short-auto-exposure leg; sustained pressure with rising miles is consistent with the thesis.

name: Regulatory milestones
whyItMatters: Governance often caps domain before engineering does.
statusPlaceholder: Internal checklist: permits, expansions, suspensions, incident responses.
interpretation: Net wider domains and shorter recovery times after incidents support deployment; repeated retrenchment or moratoria invalidates a linear scaling assumption.

name: Used passenger vehicle residuals
whyItMatters: Proxy for ownership demand and replacement behavior as utilization models shift.
statusPlaceholder: Wholesale index and segment splits; urban vs. national divergence is the focus.
interpretation: Sustained softness in relevant segments is consistent with utilization shift; broad strength would require revisiting how fast ownership erodes in target cities.

name: Fleet utilization
statusPlaceholder: Operator disclosures where available; otherwise inferred from trip and fleet count proxies.
interpretation: Rising utilization without destructive pricing supports the base path; flat utilization with rising discounts points to oversupply or weak demand.
